preamble: The phonetic transformation from the close-mid back rounded vowel /o/ to the diphthong /oi/ (typically realized as /ɔɪ/) involves a process of diphthongization. This shift introduces a front glide after the initial back rounded vowel, often accompanied by a slight lowering of the first element or a fronting of the second element of the diphthong. This type of change can occur in stressed syllables or in specific phonetic environments.
phoneticEffects: Diphthongization, fronting of the second element, possible lowering of the first element

examples.note: Diphthongization of short 'o' to 'oi', as in 'coal' pronounced as 'coil' in the industrialized West of Yorkshire.
An unpredictable sound change where final /o/ shifts to /oi/ before a suffix is added.
